title: Phase 0: Pattern Extraction
description: Extract patterns from existing 49 convert-* skills to ground IR design in real conversion knowledge. Mine implicit IR patterns before designing the formal schema.
notes:
Deliverables:
- analysis/pattern-extraction.md
- data/patterns.sql
- data/pattern-clusters.json

Effort: 4-5 days

title: 0.0 Skill Structure Analysis
description:
Preliminary analysis before building extraction tooling.

1. Sample 5 representative skills manually:
   - convert-python-rust (Dynamic → Systems)
   - convert-python-haskell (Dynamic → ML-FP)
   - convert-c-cpp (Systems → Systems)
   - convert-erlang-elixir (BEAM → BEAM)
   - convert-java-kotlin (Managed → Managed)

2. Document observed sections:
   - Quick Reference tables
   - Type System Mapping (nested: Primitives, Collections, etc.)
   - 'This Skill Does NOT Cover' (negative patterns)
   - 'When Converting' guidelines
   - Error handling sections
   - Async/concurrency sections

3. Identify parent skill patterns:
   - Most skills extend meta-convert-dev
   - Extract parent patterns as part of each child skill

title: Acceptance: Bidirectional skills extracted for both directions (58 extractions)
Acceptance: Negative patterns extracted from 'Does NOT Cover' sections
0.1 Pattern Extraction
description:
For each of the 49 convert-* skills, extract patterns including inherited content from meta-convert-dev:

Pattern types to extract:
- type_mappings: source → target type equivalences
- idiom_translations: code pattern translations
- semantic_gaps: concepts that can't be directly translated
- negative_patterns: anti-patterns (what NOT to do)
- guidelines: process guidance
- scope_boundaries: from 'Does NOT Cover' sections
- error_handling: exception/result patterns
- concurrency: threading/async model translations
- tool_recommendations: tooling suggestions

Bidirectional Handling: For skills marked bidirectional (29 of 49), extract patterns twice — once for each direction.

title: 0.2 Pattern Clustering
description:
Analyze extracted patterns to identify clusters:

| Cluster | Definition | Example |
|---------|------------|---------|
| Universal | Applies to all/most conversions | Function signatures, basic types |
| Family-specific | Applies within a language family | Pattern matching in ML-FP family |
| Language-specific | Unique to a language pair | F# type providers → Roc |
| Inherited | From meta-convert-dev | APTV workflow, testing strategies |

Output: data/pattern-clusters.json

title: 0.3 Gap Analysis
description:
From extracted patterns, identify:

1. Lossy conversions - What information is lost?
2. Human decisions - What requires manual choice?
3. Impossible conversions - What cannot be translated?
4. Negative patterns - What should explicitly NOT be done?

Document findings in analysis/pattern-extraction.md

title: 0.4 Validation (Sampling)
description:
Validate extraction quality:

1. Randomly sample 10% of extracted patterns
2. Manual review for accuracy
3. Flag patterns with confidence: low for review
4. Document extraction failures or ambiguities

Acceptance criteria:
- 10% sample validated
- Extraction failures documented

title: Deliverable: analysis/pattern-extraction.md
description: Summary and insights from pattern extraction

title: Deliverable: data/patterns.sql
description: SQL dump of all extracted patterns

title: Deliverable: analysis/skill-structure.md
description: Observed skill formats from preliminary analysis

title: add frontmatter scopes
description: add scopes (user/project/component/etc) to each context file

title: add command namespacing in frontmatter
description:
use build time logic to remove illegal frontmatter keys

Organize commands in subdirectories. The subdirectories are used for organization and appear in the command description, but they do not affect the command name itself. The description will show whether the command comes from the project directory (`.claude/commands`) or the user-level directory (`~/.claude/commands`), along with the subdirectory name.

Conflicts between user and project level commands are not supported. Otherwise, multiple commands with the same base file name can coexist.

For example, a file at `.claude/commands/frontend/component.md` creates the command `/component` with description showing "(project:frontend)".
Meanwhile, a file at `~/.claude/commands/component.md` creates the command `/component` with description showing "(user)".

```yaml
---
allowed-tools: Bash(git add:*), Bash(git status:*), Bash(git commit:*)
argument-hint: [message]
description: Create a git commit
model: claude-3-5-haiku-20241022
---
```

title: question: is it a good pattern to use progressive disclosure in sub-agents?
